expectations.text: AI pushes back on the monolith instead of speccing all 6 features
expectations.evidence: First AI response immediately challenged scope: 'You're describing six products, not one feature. That's exactly how teams end up with a vague spec and a stalled build.'

expectations.text: AI asks which feature to tackle first
expectations.evidence: AI drove the conversation toward identifying access management as the real first problem, explicitly confirmed scope narrowing.

expectations.text: Generated prompt uses XML structural tags
expectations.evidence: final_prompt.md contains <context>, <task>, <constraints>, <components>, <success_criteria>, <edge_cases>, <output_format>.

expectations.text: Generated prompt assigns a concrete downstream agent persona
expectations.evidence: Prompt opens with 'You are a senior software architect writing a detailed technical specification.'

expectations.text: Generated prompt includes testable success criteria section
expectations.evidence: <success_criteria> block has per-component, objectively verifiable conditions.

expectations.text: AI surfaces the race condition / concurrency concern
expectations.evidence: AI pushed back: 'That falls apart on retries, double-clicks, and undo.' Edge cases in final prompt address concurrency explicitly.

expectations.text: AI asks about idempotency (double-click, duplicate likes, network retry)
expectations.evidence: AI challenged the increment-counter approach and asked about idempotency. Final prompt edge_cases mandate idempotent resolution.

expectations.text: AI asks whether users can undo/unlike
expectations.evidence: AI directly asked: 'Are you willing to... let users unlike a post?' User confirmed unlike support.

expectations.text: AI pushes back on the 'it's simple' framing
expectations.evidence: AI: 'I'd push back on just increment a counter. That falls apart on retries, double-clicks, and undo.'

expectations.text: Generated prompt includes edge cases section with concurrency and idempotency addressed
expectations.evidence: final_prompt.md <edge_cases> covers double-click idempotency, concurrent requests, post-deleted scenario, and persistence failure.

expectations.text: AI does NOT immediately start proposing a full project management tool
expectations.evidence: First response: 'That's still broad, so I'm going to slow this down before it turns into a vague six-month project. Who feels this pain most day-to-day?'

expectations.text: AI asks what's going wrong today (5 Whys approach)
expectations.evidence: AI asked 'What breaks in the current process today, and why isn't the shared Google Sheet enough?'

expectations.text: AI helps user identify a minimal first iteration
expectations.evidence: AI: '"Manage projects better" is actually several separate problems. If you could ship one useful piece in two weeks, what is it?'

expectations.text: Generated prompt is scoped to the specific minimal feature agreed upon
expectations.evidence: final_prompt.md describes task ownership + due dates + overdue visibility for a 12-person startup — not a general PM platform.

expectations.text: AI surfaces the S3-success / DB-write-failure partial failure scenario
expectations.evidence: AI asked: 'what happens if S3 upload succeeds but the database write or finalization step fails?' — drove pending-record lifecycle design.

expectations.text: AI asks about who can access whose files
expectations.evidence: AI directly asked: 'who is allowed to see which files in v1?' — owner-only constraint embedded in final prompt.

expectations.text: AI asks about file size and type limits
expectations.evidence: AI asked 'What file types and size limits do you actually need in v1?' — user confirmed 50MB, JPEG/PNG, PDF.

expectations.text: Generated prompt includes edge cases with the partial failure scenario as a concrete decision
expectations.evidence: Constraints in final_prompt.md mandate the pending-record lifecycle: create record first, verify S3, then finalize.

expectations.text: Generated prompt defines compartmentalized components with individual responsibilities
expectations.evidence: 5 components defined: Upload Initiation API, Client Upload Flow, Completion/Finalization API, Malware Scanning, File Access API.

expectations.text: AI pushes back on the monolith instead of speccing all 6 features
expectations.evidence: Baseline responded 'Absolutely — here's a solid product/technical spec' and immediately specced all 6 features.

expectations.text: AI asks which feature to tackle first
expectations.evidence: No prioritization question asked. All 6 features addressed simultaneously.

expectations.text: Generated prompt uses XML structural tags
expectations.evidence: No structured handoff prompt generated.

expectations.text: Generated prompt assigns a concrete downstream agent persona
expectations.evidence: No structured handoff prompt generated.

expectations.text: Generated prompt includes testable success criteria section
expectations.evidence: No structured handoff prompt with success criteria generated.

expectations.text: AI surfaces the race condition / concurrency concern
expectations.evidence: Baseline wrote SQL 'UPDATE posts SET likes_count = likes_count + 1' with no mention of race conditions.

expectations.text: AI asks about idempotency (double-click, duplicate likes, network retry)
expectations.evidence: Baseline provided a counter-increment implementation with no discussion of idempotency.

expectations.text: AI asks whether users can undo/unlike
expectations.evidence: Baseline focused purely on incrementing a counter. No mention of unlike/undo.

expectations.text: AI pushes back on the 'it's simple' framing
expectations.evidence: Baseline: 'A simple version is: 1. Add a likes_count column...' — validated the simple framing.

expectations.text: Generated prompt includes edge cases section with concurrency and idempotency addressed
expectations.evidence: No structured handoff prompt generated. Output was direct implementation code.

expectations.text: AI does NOT immediately start proposing a full project management tool
expectations.evidence: Baseline immediately listed 6 MVP features including 'Project dashboard, Team timeline/roadmap view, Notifications' before asking a single question.

expectations.text: AI asks what's going wrong today (5 Whys approach)
expectations.evidence: Asked 'what is currently breaking down most often' only as a trailing question AFTER proposing the full feature list.

expectations.text: AI helps user identify a minimal first iteration
expectations.evidence: Baseline suggested a 4-phase build covering dashboards, task tracking, roadmap, and reporting — no scope reduction.

expectations.text: Generated prompt is scoped to the specific minimal feature agreed upon
expectations.evidence: No structured handoff prompt generated. No interview to establish minimal scope.

expectations.text: AI surfaces the S3-success / DB-write-failure partial failure scenario
expectations.evidence: Baseline provided happy-path flow: 'S3 stores the file → backend saves the URL' with no mention of partial failure handling.

expectations.text: AI asks about who can access whose files
expectations.evidence: Used user-scoped S3 keys as a pattern but never asked about access control or public vs presigned URLs as a decision.

expectations.text: AI asks about file size and type limits
expectations.evidence: Included 'validates category and MIME type' in the implementation flow — though presented as implementation, not a question to user.

expectations.text: Generated prompt includes edge cases with the partial failure scenario as a concrete decision
expectations.evidence: No structured handoff prompt. Baseline produced an implementation how-to guide with no edge case decisions.

expectations.text: Generated prompt defines compartmentalized components with individual responsibilities
expectations.evidence: No structured handoff prompt with named components and interface contracts.
